commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=40a546e700a32dc8cb1b9db5c74b89169b4907a4)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds optional `status`, `code`, and `errorType` signals to provider failover hook contexts, forwards them through the existing failover hook path, normalizes nested error types, and adds focused tests.

PR surface: Source +111, Tests +84. Total +195 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a reported bug. Source inspection confirms current main cannot pass structured provider `status`, `code`, or `errorType` through this hook path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual maintainer handling is needed because this changes a public provider hook contract and the remaining proof gap cannot be supplied safely by an automated repair job.

target.title: feat: pass structured provider error signals to hooks
target.body:
## Summary

- Rewrites the provider error-descriptor proposal into the existing failover hook path instead of adding a new SDK subpath or descriptor layer.
- Extends provider failover contexts with structured `status`, `code`, and `errorType` fields and forwards them to provider-owned classification hooks.
- Normalizes nested provider error types before failover classification so ambiguous 401/403 responses can be refined by stable provider codes.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors-provider-structured-signals.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/provider-error-patterns.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts`
- `git diff origin/main --check`
- `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: provider hooks can classify ambiguous structured provider failures, such as a 403 with a quota/billing code, without core adding provider-specific descriptor policy.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on the PR branch rebased onto current `origin/main`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors-provider-structured-signals.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/provider-error-patterns.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts`.
Evidence after fix: focused agents/plugins shards passed; SDK subpath export check passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: structured `status`, `code`, and `errorType` reach provider failover hooks and can refine generic auth statuses to billing/rate-limit reasons.
What was not tested: live upstream provider responses. The broader core TS lane currently fails on unchanged `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts` nullable number errors on `origin/main`.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
